show.title: Grateful Dead - Cornell 5/8/77 FULL SHOW | AI-Generated Visual Experience (Betty Board SBD)
show.description:
Grateful Dead - May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
The complete show, widely regarded as the greatest Grateful Dead concert ever performed.

This is Dead Air -- a custom AI-generated visual experience synchronized to the legendary Betty Boards soundboard recording. Every song features real-time reactive visuals inspired by the liquid light shows and concert lighting of the era, reimagined through generative art and AI.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)
Added to the National Recording Registry by the Library of Congress in 2012.

--- SET I ---
0:00:00 New Minglewood Blues
0:05:30 Loser
0:12:30 El Paso
0:17:00 They Love Each Other
0:24:00 Jack Straw
0:29:30 Deal
0:35:00 Lazy Lightnin' > Supplication
0:44:00 Brown Eyed Women
0:49:30 Mama Tried
0:52:30 Row Jimmy
1:02:00 Dancin' in the Streets

--- SET II ---
1:14:00 Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
1:35:00 Estimated Prophet
1:48:00 St. Stephen > Not Fade Away
2:06:00 Drums / Space
2:18:00 Morning Dew
2:30:00 One More Saturday Night

(Timestamps are approximate -- will be updated with exact times.)

The Spring '77 tour is the gold standard of Grateful Dead performance. The band was at peak chemistry: Jerry Garcia's tone was crystalline, Bob Weir's rhythm work locked in tight with Keith Godchaux's piano runs, Phil Lesh was pushing boundaries on bass, and the Donna Jean harmonies soared. Betty Cantor-Jackson's soundboard capture preserves it all with remarkable clarity.

This visual accompaniment was created with Dead Air, a project exploring AI-driven concert visuals for archival live music. The visuals are generated in real time from audio analysis -- no pre-rendered video, no stock footage. Just the music driving the art.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #DeadAir
tags: Scarlet Begonias Fire on the Mountain

s1t02.title: New Minglewood Blues - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s1t02.description:
New Minglewood Blues - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 1, Track 1 -- Show Opener

The show that needs no introduction opens with a fiery Minglewood. Bob Weir tears into it with swagger, setting the tone for what would become the most celebrated Grateful Dead concert in history.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording. The reactive concert lighting and liquid light effects are driven entirely by real-time audio analysis. No stock footage, no pre-rendered video.

This show was added to the National Recording Registry by the Library of Congress in 2012, the first rock concert recording to receive the honor.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #NewMinglewoodBlues #DeadAir

s1t03.title: Loser - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s1t03.description:
Loser - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 1, Track 2

Jerry Garcia delivers a deeply felt Loser early in the first set. His vocal phrasing and guitar tone on this version are textbook '77 Garcia -- warm, precise, emotionally present. The line "I got no chance of losing this time" has never sounded more convincing.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated liquid light visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording. Every visual element responds to the music in real time.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #Loser #JerryGarcia #DeadAir

s1t04.title: El Paso - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s1t04.description:
El Paso - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 1, Track 3

The Marty Robbins classic gets the Dead treatment. A set one staple of the era, this tight arrangement showcases the band's country-rock roots and Bob Weir's affection for cowboy songs. Short and sweet, exactly as it should be.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated concert lighting visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording.

s1t05.title: They Love Each Other - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s1t05.description:
They Love Each Other - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 1, Track 4

TLEO in its refined 1977 arrangement -- uptempo, joyful, with Garcia's lead lines dancing over the rhythm section. By '77 this song had evolved from its earlier folk-rock origins into a tight, propulsive vehicle. The interplay between Jerry and Keith Godchaux's piano is a highlight.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated liquid light visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #TheyLoveEachOther #TLEO #DeadAir

s1t06.title: Jack Straw - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s1t06.description:
Jack Straw - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 1, Track 5

One of the great Weir-Garcia vocal duets, Jack Straw is a murder ballad wrapped in gorgeous harmonies. This version crackles with energy -- the dual vocal interplay is locked in, and the band builds to a ferocious climax. A quintessential '77 Jack Straw.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated concert lighting visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording.

s1t07.title: Deal - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s1t07.description:
Deal - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 1, Track 6

Deal is a Garcia/Hunter classic that always brought the energy up. This version is no exception -- Jerry's solo builds with purpose and fire, the band locks into the groove, and the crowd knows every word. A reliable set-one powerhouse that delivers here with authority.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

s1t08.title: Lazy Lightnin' > Supplication - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s1t08.description:
Lazy Lightnin' > Supplication - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 1, Tracks 7-8

The inseparable Weir pair -- Lazy Lightnin' always segued into Supplication, and this version is a gem. The quirky, angular Lazy Lightnin' gives way to the soaring, expansive Supplication jam where the whole band stretches out. Phil Lesh's bass work during the Supplication jam is particularly adventurous. One of the great underrated pairings in the Dead's repertoire.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording. The visual modes shift from concert lighting into liquid light as the segue unfolds.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #LazyLightnin #Supplication #BobWeir #DeadAir

s1t10.title: Brown Eyed Women - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s1t10.description:
Brown Eyed Women - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 1, Track 9

A top-tier Brown Eyed Women. Garcia's storytelling vocal is fully engaged, and the band swings with that unmistakable '77 pocket. The guitar solo is melodic and purposeful -- Jerry at his narrative best, every note in service of the song. This is one of the versions that makes best-of lists for good reason.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated oil projector visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording.

s1t11.title: Mama Tried - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s1t11.description:
Mama Tried - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 1, Track 10

The Merle Haggard honky-tonk classic, delivered straight and true by Bob Weir. A compact breather between heavier material, Mama Tried was a staple of Dead first sets throughout the '70s. No frills, just country-rock done right.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated concert lighting visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording.

s1t12.title: Row Jimmy - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s1t12.description:
Row Jimmy - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 1, Track 11

Row Jimmy is one of the Dead's most hypnotic songs -- a slow, swaying meditation that seems to exist outside of time. This Cornell version is languid and beautiful, with Garcia's voice floating over the gently pulsing rhythm. The extended arrangement lets the band drift into a dreamlike space. A masterclass in the Dead's ability to make stillness as compelling as frenzy.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording, shifting between liquid light, oil projector, and lo-fi grain modes as the song evolves.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #RowJimmy #JerryGarcia #DeadAir

s1t13.title: Dancin' in the Streets - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 -- Set 1 Closer (Betty Board SBD)
s1t13.description:
Dancin' in the Streets - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 1, Track 12 -- Set Closer

The first set closer, and what a closer it is. The Dead's extended reworking of the Martha and the Vandellas hit was a signature vehicle for exploratory jamming in the mid-to-late '70s. This version builds from the familiar riff into an expansive, driving jam that takes the crowd to the edge before the setbreak. The energy in Barton Hall is palpable even through the soundboard.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording, cycling through concert lighting, liquid light, and oil projector modes as the jam develops.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #DancinInTheStreets #SetCloser #DeadAir

s2t02.title: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s2t02.description:
Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 2, Tracks 1-2 -- Set Opener

The legendary Scarlet > Fire. While the pairing was still relatively new in May '77 (it had only been linked a handful of times before this show), this version helped cement it as one of the most iconic segues in rock history. The Scarlet jam builds with patient intensity before dissolving into the hypnotic pulse of Fire on the Mountain. This is arguably the version that defined what Scarlet > Fire could be.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording. The visuals shift from liquid light during Scarlet into concert lighting for Fire, with oil projector and lo-fi grain textures woven through the jam.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #ScarletBegonias #FireOnTheMountain #ScarletFire #DeadAir

s2t04.title: Estimated Prophet - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s2t04.description:
Estimated Prophet - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 2, Track 3

Estimated Prophet was brand new in 1977, and this early version is already fully formed and magnificent. The song's unusual 7/4 time signature gives it a lurching, prophetic quality, and Bob Weir's increasingly unhinged vocal performance builds to the cathartic "My time coming" climax. The jam section pushes into deep, strange territory -- the band sounds like they're channeling something from beyond. One of the definitive early Estimated Prophets.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording, featuring liquid light and particle nebula modes for the extended jam.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #EstimatedProphet #BobWeir #DeadAir

s2t05.title: St. Stephen > Not Fade Away - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 -- Rare '77 Stephen! (Betty Board SBD)
s2t05.description:
St. Stephen > Not Fade Away - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 2, Tracks 4-5

St. Stephen was a rarity by 1977 -- it had been shelved since 1971 and was only brought back a handful of times during the Spring '77 tour. Its reappearance at Cornell sent shockwaves through Barton Hall. The explosive arrangement segues seamlessly into Not Fade Away, which the band rides with a driving, Bo Diddley-beat intensity before heading into Drums/Space.

The crowd reaction when they recognize the opening notes of St. Stephen is one of the great moments in Dead lore.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated concert lighting visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #StStephen #NotFadeAway #NFA #DeadAir

The Bo Diddley beat pulses through Barton Hall as the band drives NFA with relentless energy. See the combined St. Stephen > Not Fade Away upload for the full unbroken segue. This version rides the groove deep before dissolving into the Drums/Space segment.

s2t07.title: Drums / Space - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 (Betty Board SBD)
s2t07.description:
Drums / Space - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 2, Track 6

The nightly ritual. Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart take the stage alone for the percussion exploration before the full band dissolves into the freeform void of Space. The Cornell Drums/Space serves as the bridge between the explosive St. Stephen > NFA and the transcendent Morning Dew that follows. The journey from rhythm to abstraction and back is the Dead at their most experimental.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording, featuring particle nebula, stark minimal, and oil projector modes for the abstract passages.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #Drums #Space #DrumsSpace #DeadAir

s2t08.title: Morning Dew - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 -- One of the All-Time Great Versions (Betty Board SBD)
s2t08.description:
Morning Dew - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 2, Track 7

This is it. The Cornell '77 Morning Dew is frequently cited as the single greatest performance of any song in the Grateful Dead's entire catalog. Emerging from Space, Jerry Garcia builds the post-apocalyptic ballad from a whisper to an overwhelming crescendo. His guitar work in the final build is devastating -- each phrase more intense than the last, reaching a peak of emotional expression that is almost unbearable in its beauty. The crowd erupts. Garcia's voice cracks with the weight of what the band has just channeled.

If you only listen to one Dead song in your life, many would tell you to make it this one.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated liquid light and concert lighting visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording. The visuals build in parallel with Garcia's solo, reaching full intensity at the climax.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #MorningDew #JerryGarcia #GOAT #DeadAir

s2t09.title: One More Saturday Night - Grateful Dead | Cornell 5/8/77 -- Encore (Betty Board SBD)
s2t09.description:
One More Saturday Night - Grateful Dead
May 8, 1977 | Barton Hall, Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Set 2, Track 8 -- Encore

The encore. After the emotional devastation of Morning Dew, Weir brings the crowd back to earth with the party-starting One More Saturday Night. Except it was a Sunday. Classic Dead. The energy is celebratory -- everyone in Barton Hall knows they just witnessed something extraordinary, and this Chuck Berry-style rocker is the joyful release valve.

Source: SBD > Master Reel > Cassette (Betty Cantor-Jackson Boards)

This is Dead Air -- AI-generated concert lighting visuals synchronized to the original soundboard recording.

#GratefulDead #Cornell77 #OneMoreSaturdayNight #OMSN #Encore #BobWeir #DeadAir
